title: Caralluma geminata
taxonomy: Caralluma geminata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Caralluma, which contains over 100 species of succulent plants. The genus Caralluma is further classified under the subfamily Periplocoideae, which is a group of plants characterized by their unique flowers and growth habits. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics, which have been studied by botanists and taxonomists. Caralluma geminata is native to the tropical regions of the world, where it grows as a succulent plant.

intro: Caralluma geminata is a type of succulent plant belonging to the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of India and Sri Lanka. The health benefits of Caralluma geminata include its potential to aid in weight loss, improve glucose metabolism, and reduce inflammation.
